title: The Writer's Journey
author: Christopher Vogler
source: Originally a 7-page Disney memo (c. 1985); expanded to The Writer's Journey: Mythic Structure for Writers (1992).
lineage_text: Practical screenwriting adaptation of Joseph Campbell's monomyth (Hero with a Thousand Faces, 1949). Where Campbell is descriptive and anthropological, Vogler is prescriptive and production-ready.

lineage.note: Practical screenwriting adaptation of Campbell. The version that actually runs Hollywood: it shaped the Disney Renaissance and the modern blockbuster.

description: Twelve stages organized in three movements (Departure, Initiation, Return). Each stage names a recurring narrative function rather than a specific event — the function can be served by any number of scenes.

stages.name: Ordinary World
stages.function: Establish the hero's normal state — the world they're about to leave. Plant what's at risk.

stages.name: Call to Adventure
stages.function: A disruption demands action. The status quo is no longer viable.

stages.name: Refusal of the Call
stages.function: Hesitation, fear, the weight of the old life. The cost of leaving is felt.

stages.name: Meeting the Mentor
stages.function: A guide appears — wisdom, training, a gift. May be a person, a teaching, or an internal realization.

stages.name: Crossing the First Threshold
stages.function: The hero commits. Past this point, returning to the old life is no longer possible.

stages.name: Tests, Allies, Enemies
stages.function: The hero learns the rules of the new world. Friendships, rivalries, and small victories accumulate.

stages.name: Approach to the Inmost Cave
stages.function: Preparation for the central ordeal. Plans laid, allies gathered, fears named.

stages.name: The Ordeal
stages.function: The midpoint crisis. A death-and-rebirth moment — the hero faces their greatest fear.

stages.name: The Reward (Seizing the Sword)
stages.function: Surviving the ordeal, the hero gains something — knowledge, an object, a new self. The price may not yet be clear.

stages.name: The Road Back
stages.function: The hero commits to returning to the ordinary world. Often a chase or a renewed pursuit.

stages.name: The Resurrection
stages.function: Final test — the highest stakes, the deepest transformation. The hero is reborn into the new self.

stages.name: Return with the Elixir
stages.function: The hero brings home what the journey produced — wisdom, a cure, freedom, a new equilibrium for the community.
